ucs.title: [LLM] PLATINUM Dipsind/JPIN ICMP C2 — non-LOLBIN process generating sustained ICMP egress
ucs.description: Hunts for PLATINUM's hallmark covert C2 channel — the Dipsind/JPIN backdoor families documented by Microsoft (April 2016) used raw ICMP (T1095 Non-Application Layer Protocol) instead of HTTP/S to blend with network noise on South/Southeast Asian government networks. Legitimate ICMP almost exclusively comes from ping.exe/tracert.exe/kernel; sustained ICMP from any other process to a public IP is characteristic of this actor.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen dc(All_Traffic.dest) as dest_count values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.src) as src_host from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.transport=icmp All_Traffic.dest_category!=internal NOT All_Traffic.app IN ("ping","tracert","pathping","system") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.app All_Traffic.process_name span=1m | `drop_dm_object_name("All_Traffic")` | stats count as buckets sum(count) as packet_count values(dest_ip) as dest_ip values(src) as src by app process_name | where buckets>=5 AND packet_count>=20 | sort - packet_count
ucs.kql:
// PLATINUM (G0068) — Dipsind/JPIN-style ICMP C2 channel
let KnownIcmpBins = dynamic(["ping.exe","tracert.exe","pathping.exe","system","ntoskrnl.exe"]);
let WindowsSysPaths = dynamic([@"c:\windows\system32\", @"c:\windows\syswow64\"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where Protocol == "Icmp"                                    // raw ICMP only
| where RemoteIPType == "Public"                              // skip LAN ping sweeps
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (KnownIcmpBins)
| where not(tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath) has_any (WindowsSysPaths))
| where isnotempty(InitiatingProcessFileName)
| summarize PacketCount      = count(),
            DistinctMinutes  = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1m)),
            DistinctHours    = dcount(bin(Timestamp, 1h)),
            FirstSeen        = min(Timestamp),
            LastSeen         = max(Timestamp),
            Hosts            = make_set(DeviceName, 50),
            DestIPs          = make_set(RemoteIP, 50),
            SampleCmd        = any(InitiatingProcessCommandLine),
            SampleSHA256     = any(InitiatingProcessSHA256)
            by InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where DistinctMinutes >= 5      // sustained beacon cadence — Dipsind keepalive
   and PacketCount > 20           // strip one-off ICMP probes
| order by DistinctHours desc, PacketCount desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// PLATINUM (G0068) — ICMP C2 — ASIM port; covers MDE + Sysmon + Linux audit
let KnownIcmpBins = dynamic(["ping.exe","tracert.exe","pathping.exe","ping","system"]);
ImNetworkSession
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where NetworkProtocol =~ "ICMP"
| where isnotempty(DstIpAddr)
| where not(ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr))
| where not(DstIpAddr startswith "127.") and not(DstIpAddr startswith "169.254.")
| extend ProcLeaf = tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where isnotempty(ActingProcessName)
| where ProcLeaf !in~ (KnownIcmpBins)
| where not(tolower(ActingProcessName) startswith @"c:\windows\system32\")
   and not(tolower(ActingProcessName) startswith @"c:\windows\syswow64\")
| summarize PacketCount     = count(),
            DistinctMinutes = dcount(bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)),
            FirstSeen       = min(TimeGenerated),
            LastSeen        = max(TimeGenerated),
            Hosts           = make_set(DvcHostname, 50),
            DestIPs         = make_set(DstIpAddr, 50),
            SampleCmd       = any(ActingProcessCommandLine)
            by ProcLeaf, ActingProcessName
| where DistinctMinutes >= 5 and PacketCount > 20
| order by PacketCount desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Suspicious ICMP Egress From Non-Standard Process (PLATINUM Dipsind)
id: 7d3b4f1a-9b8a-4e2c-9a4f-platinumicmpc2
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects raw ICMP traffic to public IPs from a process that is not a known
  ping/trace utility — characteristic of the PLATINUM (G0068) Dipsind/JPIN
  backdoor families which use ICMP for C2 to evade web-proxy inspection.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0068/
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2016/04/26/digging-deep-for-platinum/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1095
  - attack.g0068
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Protocol: 'icmp'
    DestinationIsIpv4: true
  filter_known_bins:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\ping.exe'
      - '\tracert.exe'
      - '\pathping.exe'
  filter_system_paths:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
      - 'C:\Windows\SysWOW64\'
  filter_private_dest:
    DestinationIp|startswith:
      - '10.'
      - '192.168.'
      - '172.16.'
      - '127.'
      - '169.254.'
  condition: selection and not (filter_known_bins or filter_system_paths or filter_private_dest)
falsepositives:
  - Custom monitoring agents that perform ICMP probes
  - Network management software running outside System32
level: high
ucs.rationale: ICMP-based C2 is rare across all APTs but is a documented signature behaviour of PLATINUM's bespoke Dipsind backdoor (Microsoft DCU/MMPC report April 2016). Almost no legitimate Windows process besides ping/tracert and the kernel emits raw ICMP — the combination of (a) public-IP destination, (b) non-LOLBIN initiator, and (c) sustained cadence (≥5 distinct minutes) excludes troubleshooting use. Gen

_sentinel_field_issues.message: `ActingProcessName` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.
`ActingProcessCommandLine` is not a column of ImNetworkSession; it does exist on ImProcessCreate, ImFileEvent, ImRegistryEvent.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] PLATINUM spearphish-to-LSASS chain — Office attachment → masqueraded payload → LSASS credential access
ucs.description: Hunts the full PLATINUM kill chain documented by Microsoft: T1566.001 spearphish-attachment → T1204.002 user opens lure document targeting Indian/Malaysian/Indonesian government victims → T1036 masqueraded helper binary (often dropped to %TEMP% or %APPDATA% under a benign-sounding name) → T1003.001 LSASS memory access. Correlates Office parent + non-system child + LSASS read in a 30-minute window.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as office_time values(Processes.process) as office_cmd values(Processes.process_hash) as parent_hash from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe") Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\Public\\*") NOT Processes.process_name IN ("splwow64.exe","WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_hash _time span=1s | rename Processes.* as * | join type=inner host user [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as lsass_time values(Processes.parent_process_name) as lsass_parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("rundll32.exe","comsvcs.dll","procdump.exe") OR (Processes.process="*lsass*" AND Processes.process="*MiniDump*") by host Processes.user _time span=1s | rename Processes.* as * | eval lsass_time=lsass_time] | where lsass_time>=office_time AND lsass_time<=office_time+1800 | table host user process_name process_hash office_cmd lsass_parent office_time lsass_time | sort - office_time
ucs.kql:
// PLATINUM (G0068) — Office spearphish → masqueraded helper → LSASS access chain
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let ChainWindow  = 30m;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wordpad.exe"]);
let UserDropPaths = dynamic([@"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\appdata\roaming\", @"\users\public\", @"\windows\temp\"]);
// Stage 1 — Office spawns a non-Office binary from a user-writable dir (T1204.002 + T1036)
let Stage1 =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (OfficeParents)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where tolower(FolderPath) has_any (UserDropPaths)
    | where FileName !in~ ("splwow64.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")
    | where not(tolower(FolderPath) startswith @"c:\program files")
    | project StageOneTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LureParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              LureParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              DropName = FileName, DropPath = FolderPath,
              DropSHA256 = SHA256, DropCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              DropProcessId = ProcessId;
// Stage 2 — credential access against LSASS within 30m on the same device (T1003.001)
let Stage2 =
    union
    ( DeviceProcessEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
        | where (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
            or (ProcessCommandLine has "lsass" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","procdump","-ma "))
            or FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
        | project Stage2Time = Timestamp, DeviceId, Stage2Cmd = ProcessCommandLine, Stage2File = FileName ),
    ( DeviceEvents
        | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
        | where ActionType in ("OpenProcessApiCall","ReadProcessMemoryApiCall")
        | where FileName =~ "lsass.exe" or AdditionalFields has "lsass.exe"
        | project Stage2Time = Timestamp, DeviceId,
                  Stage2Cmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
                  Stage2File = InitiatingProcessFileName );
Stage1
| join kind=inner Stage2 on DeviceId
| where Stage2Time between (StageOneTime .. StageOneTime + ChainWindow)
| project StageOneTime, Stage2Time,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Stage2Time, StageOneTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          LureParent, LureParentCmd,
          DropName, DropPath, DropSHA256, DropCmd,
          Stage2File, Stage2Cmd
| order by StageOneTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// PLATINUM (G0068) — Office → masqueraded drop → LSASS — Sentinel SecurityEvent port
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let ChainWindow  = 30m;
let OfficeParents = dynamic(["winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","wordpad.exe"]);
let UserDropPaths = dynamic([@"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\appdata\roaming\", @"\users\public\", @"\windows\temp\"]);
let Stage1 =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1])),
             ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1])),
             ChildPath  = tolower(NewProcessName)
    | where ParentLeaf in (OfficeParents)
    | where ChildPath has_any (UserDropPaths)
    | where ChildLeaf !in~ ("splwow64.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe")
    | project StageOneTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              LureParent = ParentLeaf, DropName = ChildLeaf,
              DropPath = NewProcessName, DropCmd = CommandLine;
let Stage2 =
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "rundll32.exe" and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
        or (CommandLine has "lsass" and CommandLine has_any ("MiniDump","procdump","-ma "))
        or NewProcessName endswith "procdump.exe"
        or NewProcessName endswith "procdump64.exe"
    | project Stage2Time = TimeGenerated, Computer, Stage2Cmd = CommandLine, Stage2Bin = NewProcessName;
Stage1
| join kind=inner Stage2 on Computer
| where Stage2Time between (StageOneTime .. StageOneTime + ChainWindow)
| project StageOneTime, Stage2Time,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Stage2Time, StageOneTime),
          Computer, Account, LureParent, DropName, DropPath, DropCmd,
          Stage2Bin, Stage2Cmd
| order by StageOneTime desc
ucs.rationale: The Microsoft DCU PLATINUM whitepaper documents this exact sequence as their primary intrusion pattern against Indian/Malaysian/Indonesian government targets — weaponised Office attachments dropping helpers to user-writable directories that subsequently scrape LSASS for credential reuse. Detecting either stage in isolation produces noise (red-teamers also drop Office macros, IT support uses procdu
